skill.name: git-push
skill.description: 一键推送项目到 GitHub。自动扫描大文件、生成 .gitignore、初始化 Git、创建仓库并推送。支持首次推送、日常更新、版本发布三种模式。
skill.summary: One-click GitHub deployment with automatic large file scanning, .gitignore generation, and release management.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 69 patterns as 'external_commands' but these are FALSE POSITIVE - the skill file is Markdown documentation using backticks for code formatting, not executable Ruby code. High-severity findings about .env and certificate file access are intentional security features that scan and exclude sensitive files. The skill uses standard git/gh CLI commands safely with user confirmation for risky operations. Minor risk: executes shell commands via git/gh CLI which is inherent to git automation tools.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via Git/CLI
low_findings.description: Skill executes git and gh CLI commands for repository operations. This is expected behavior for a git automation tool but requires trust in the underlying commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Skill explicitly documents git init, commit, push, and gh repo create commands - standard git workflow with inherent trust in CLI tools.

content.user_title: Deploy Projects to GitHub with One Click
content.value_statement: Stop struggling with complex git workflows and accidental sensitive file commits. This skill automates the entire GitHub deployment process with built-in security scanning, large file detection, and intelligent .gitignore generation.
actual_capabilities: Scans project for large files (>10MB) and prompts for exclusion decisions
Detects sensitive files (.env, certificates, credentials) and prevents accidental commits
Generates and updates .gitignore based on project analysis
Handles first-time setup, daily updates, and version releases with appropriate workflows
Creates GitHub releases with automatic changelog generation from commit history
Manages git initialization, remote association, and push operations end-to-end
limitations: Requires git and GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed on the system
Requires valid GitHub authentication via gh auth login
Cannot bypass GitHub's 100MB single file size limit - large files must be excluded
Destructive operations like repository recreation require explicit user confirmation

use_cases.title: First-Time Project Deployment
use_cases.description: New developers can safely push projects to GitHub without worrying about exposing secrets or hitting file size limits. The skill handles environment checks, .gitignore generation, and repository creation automatically.
use_cases.target_user: Junior developers and non-technical users new to git

use_cases.title: Regular Development Workflow
use_cases.description: Quick daily commits and pushes with automatic detection of new large files. Skips unnecessary prompts for streamlined operation during active development.

use_cases.title: Release Management
use_cases.description: Creates version tags and GitHub releases with auto-generated changelogs from commit history. Supports attaching downloadable assets to releases.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainers and software publishers

prompt_templates.title: Quick Push (Beginner)
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to quickly deploy their project without thinking about technical details

prompt_templates.title: Create New Release (Intermediate)
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new release version with a changelog
prompt_templates.scenario: User has completed a milestone and wants to tag and publish a new version

prompt_templates.title: Daily Update (Intermediate)
prompt_templates.prompt: Commit and push today's changes to GitHub
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer wants to sync local work with remote repository after a coding session

prompt_templates.title: Full Deploy with Release (Advanced)
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy this project to a new private repository and create the first release version with release notes
prompt_templates.scenario: User is launching a new project and wants complete setup including initial version tagging

output_examples.output: Environment check passed: git, gh CLI, authentication all configured. Scanned project and found 2 large files (slides/ 85MB, video.mp4 120MB). Added 85MB file to .gitignore after confirmation. Created private repository 'my-project', initialized git, and pushed successfully. Repository: https://github.com/user/my-project

Found 5 commits since last version. Generated changelog: 'Added user authentication, fixed login bug, improved performance'. Tag v1.0.0 created and pushed. Release published at https://github.com/user/repo/releases/tag/v1.0.0

best_practices: Review the generated .gitignore carefully before pushing to ensure no sensitive files are included
Use private repositories by default for projects containing any potentially sensitive code or configuration
Create version releases at meaningful milestones to track project progress and provide download points
anti_patterns: Do not bypass large file warnings - files over 100MB will cause push failures and bloat your repository
Avoid using the 'delete and recreate repository' option unless absolutely necessary - this destroys all git history permanently
Do not skip the sensitive file review when creating public repositories - API keys and credentials have been accidentally exposed this way

faq.question: What happens if I accidentally try to push a file over 100MB?
faq.answer: The skill will detect it during scanning and add it to .gitignore automatically. GitHub rejects any single file over 100MB, and once a large file enters git history it's difficult to remove completely.

faq.question: Do I need to install git and gh CLI separately?
faq.answer: Yes. Install git from git-scm.com and GitHub CLI via 'brew install gh' (macOS) or from cli.github.com. Then run 'gh auth login' to authenticate.

faq.question: Can this skill push to an existing repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill detects existing repositories and can associate your local project with them. It will prompt if a repository with the same name already exists.

faq.question: How does the skill handle merge conflicts during push?
faq.answer: If the remote has commits you don't have locally, the skill will attempt 'git pull --rebase' automatically. If conflicts occur, it will ask whether you want to force push (which overwrites remote changes).

faq.question: What information does this skill have access to?
faq.answer: The skill reads your project files to scan for large and sensitive content, and executes git/gh commands on your behalf. It only communicates with GitHub for repository operations.

faq.question: Can I customize the commit messages?
faq.answer: The skill generates descriptive commit messages automatically based on your changes. For custom messages, specify them in your prompt when asking for a push.
